pat-2026-04-06-001.name: When checking whether Brian sent something to Alex on Slack,
pat-2026-04-06-001.pattern: When checking whether Brian sent something to Alex on Slack, look for short acknowledgment messages like Thanks, will take a look or Thanks Brian - will review as confirmation that Brian sent something immediately prior. Do not flag an item as open on Brian without first scanning for these receipt patterns in the DM thread. Brian corrected me twice on this - highest value signal.
pat-2026-04-06-001.problem: Brian said he sent Fasanara v2 comments to Alex on Slack. I had flagged it as an open item on Brian twice, saying the gap was real. The evidence was there all along: Alex sent a 4:46 PM DM saying Thanks Brian - will take a look and come back, which is a clear receipt acknowledgment. I missed it both times and made Brian defend himself.

pat-2026-04-06-002.name: During meeting prep, whenever a Slack message has a file att
pat-2026-04-06-002.pattern: During meeting prep, whenever a Slack message has a file attached (has:file), ALWAYS fetch and read the file using fetch_doc.py. Do not assume what the file contains based on the message text. Files attached to Slack messages are often the most important context for understanding what was communicated and whether a request was fulfilled. This applies to Drive links shared in Slack too.
pat-2026-04-06-002.problem: During meeting prep, I identified that Brian sent Alex a file on Slack at 4:15 PM Apr 2. I noted the file was sent but did not fetch and read it. Brian had to explicitly tell me to check the file. When I finally read it, it turned out to be the 3-lender term sheet comparison doc (NB vs Gramercy vs Fasanara), not a Fasanara-specific markup as I had assumed. This changed the entire picture of what Alex was acknowledging and what the open item actually is.

pat-2026-04-07-003.name: Skill guidance updated based on experience
pat-2026-04-07-003.pattern: Skill guidance updated based on experience
pat-2026-04-07-003.solution: Replaced: NEVER send emails directly — only create drafts for the user to review... -> NEVER send emails directly — only create drafts for the user to review. ALWAYS r...

pat-2026-04-07-004.name: The default `draft` command is reply-to-sender only. For rep
pat-2026-04-07-004.pattern: The default `draft` command is reply-to-sender only. For reply-all, use the patched tool at C:/redshift-bot/deer-flow/backend/.deer-flow/threads/2b2c44e3-f07d-42d0-ba3c-4d5226079bd5/user-data/workspace/gmail_tool_patched.py with --reply-all flag and PYTHONPATH=C:/redshift-bot/deer-flow/skills/custom/_shared prefix. Brian expects reply-all by default on all email drafts.
pat-2026-04-07-004.problem: Brian asked for reply-all on an email thread. The built-in `draft` command only replies to the From address. Had to write a patched gmail_tool with --reply-all flag that collects From+To+CC, deduplicates, excludes my own address, and sets To=first recipient, CC=rest. Must run with PYTHONPATH=C:/redshift-bot/deer-flow/skills/custom/_shared.

pat-2026-04-07-005.name: present_files and upload_to_drive are not interchangeable —
pat-2026-04-07-005.pattern: present_files and upload_to_drive are not interchangeable — Drive upload is required for user access across sessions and devices. Never skip it.
pat-2026-04-07-005.problem: Created a plan document and presented it locally but did not upload to Drive. User had to ask where it was.
pat-2026-04-07-005.solution: Always upload to Drive immediately after writing any output file, before or alongside present_files

pat-2026-04-07-006.name: To create a true reply-all draft, run a custom Python script
pat-2026-04-07-006.pattern: To create a true reply-all draft, run a custom Python script from within C:/redshift-bot/deer-flow/skills/custom/gmail directory using: cd C:/redshift-bot/deer-flow/skills/custom/gmail && python3 -c with sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.getcwd(), '..', '_shared')). Fetch original message headers (From, To, Cc, Message-ID), combine all recipients minus my_email into To field, set In-Reply-To and References headers, and call service.users().drafts().create(). Also: if someone is @mentioned in the email body but not formally on To/Cc, look them up via Slack and add them.
pat-2026-04-07-006.problem: Reply-all draft needed with CC. The gmail_tool.py draft command only replies to the From address — it does not pull To/Cc from the original thread. The --cc flag does not exist in the tool.

pat-2026-04-08-007.name: When user says they just received something, check Gmail FIR
pat-2026-04-08-007.pattern: When user says they just received something, check Gmail FIRST before Drive. Drive may have older drafts that are not the current version. Search email with sender name + subject keywords to find the latest attachment.
pat-2026-04-08-007.problem: User asked to compare Spanish vs English Covalto term sheet. Agent searched Drive first and found an old March 17 draft, producing a misleading analysis flagging major discrepancies. The actual final documents were email attachments sent same day by Pablo Demederos.

pat-2026-04-08-008.name: gmail_tool.py draft does NOT preserve CC recipients — it onl
pat-2026-04-08-008.pattern: gmail_tool.py draft does NOT preserve CC recipients — it only replies to the From address. For reply-all, always pull full headers manually (To, Cc, Message-ID), then build a MIME message with explicit To/Cc fields and post via drafts().create(). This is non-negotiable — reply-all is Brian s default.
pat-2026-04-08-008.problem: Used gmail_tool.py draft command to reply to an email with CC recipients (Isabel, Shalom). The tool only replied to the sender (Alex), dropping the CC recipients entirely. Brian corrected this.

pat-2026-04-08-009.name: Gmail threading requires correct In-Reply-To and References
pat-2026-04-08-009.pattern: Gmail threading requires correct In-Reply-To and References headers set to the original message Message-ID. threadId alone places the draft in the thread backend but it does not appear in the UI thread view. Always fetch the original message headers first, then set In-Reply-To = original Message-ID and References = original References + Message-ID.
pat-2026-04-08-009.problem: Draft created with threadId but not appearing in thread UI

pat-2026-04-08-010.name: PowerPoint chart updates require modifying BOTH the chart XM
pat-2026-04-08-010.pattern: PowerPoint chart updates require modifying BOTH the chart XML cache AND the embedded Excel workbook blob. The XML cache (multiLvlStrCache/numCache) is ignored on open - PowerPoint re-reads from the embedded xlsx. Also: (1) noMultiLvlLbl val=1 on catAx forces integer axis labels - must set to 0; (2) TableStyleMedium9 in table1.xml overrides chart series spPr colors - use bare tableStyleInfo with no name attribute; (3) multiLvlStrRef must be converted to strRef for reliable string axis labels.
pat-2026-04-08-010.problem: Updating credit performance PPTX charts with new data

pat-2026-04-08-011.name: Always find the source SQL before writing your own. The temp
pat-2026-04-08-011.pattern: Always find the source SQL before writing your own. The template used: (1) DQ = ADB methodology (avg daily balance of dq_bucket=2 / avg daily total balance), not EOM snapshot; (2) C/O = rollforward methodology where charge-off amount is balance on min(charge_off_dt)-1 (day before flag flips), not dt=charge_off_dt; (3) dq_bucket=2 is the 30-59 DPD bucket code. Source SQL: ds_dq30_historical.sql and loc_acct_rollforward.sql in .github/sql/.
pat-2026-04-08-011.problem: Rebuilding credit performance slides - DQ and C/O numbers did not match template

pat-2026-04-20-012.name: 1. Dileep Thazhmon is CEO (not unknown title) - always check
pat-2026-04-20-012.pattern: 1. Dileep Thazhmon is CEO (not unknown title) - always check email signatures before flagging as unknown. 2. Org chart for NB is in BBVA DD folder 1.05 - Organizational Chart (Jeeves-org-chart.pdf). 3. 3-year pro forma is with Pradeep Manoharan (pradeepan@tryjeeves.com) - do not substitute 2026 forecast as a 3-year model. 4. Capital markets plans: NB replaces CIM US bridge; CIM MX SOFOM stays. Fasanara and Gramercy were competing with NB and go away. 5. For KPIs/disbursements use MBR not portfolio report rollforward. Active customer count 730 was MX-only from BB tape - wrong for global. 6. 12312025 financial statements file is NOT audited - do not label it as such. Monthly financials to CIM are in email (Jeeves Financial Reporting thread). 7. Credit Policy v2.1 (Mar 2026) is from Pablo email thread (Drive ID: 1MOLXPE3Qjt5QBW-xP5ZpCQ8pacw_WuzO). 8. Servicing and collections policy exists - do not flag as missing. 9. Org chart was discussed in same conversation - do not flag as needing HR input after already identifying it.
pat-2026-04-20-012.problem: NB diligence deck content brief - Brian corrected multiple errors

pat-2026-04-21-013.name: Brian wants an execution-layer analyst who eliminates key-ma
pat-2026-04-21-013.pattern: Brian wants an execution-layer analyst who eliminates key-man risk. Core behavioral rules: clarify first, correct openly, deliver results not process, own failures with accountability.
pat-2026-04-21-013.problem: Brian Mauck bootstrapped his analyst identity on 2026-04-20. Key framing: analyst not tool, delegation with trust, clarify before executing, push back when wrong, surface mistakes with explanation of what changed.

pat-2026-04-21-014.name: The Slides API cannot copy live chart objects between presen
pat-2026-04-21-014.pattern: The Slides API cannot copy live chart objects between presentations. getThumbnail returns whole-slide rasters with chrome/labels included. The only way to get a clean chart image is to use the contentUrl of the image element directly from the source slide (e.g. g3cbe697f5a9_6_3 for the DQ chart). Even then, the image must be sized to match the NB deck panel dimensions exactly. The correct workflow: (1) get element contentUrl from source slide, (2) calculate target panel dimensions from NB deck layout, (3) insert image sized to fill panel. Do NOT use getThumbnail for panel-level embedding - it includes the full slide chrome. Also: updatePageElementTransform with scaleX/scaleY distorts panel fill colors - never use it to resize panel backgrounds.
pat-2026-04-21-014.problem: Tried to embed corp deck visuals into NB diligence deck via Slides API. Spent 30+ tool calls fighting panel sizing, z-order, and image embedding.

pat-2026-04-21-015.name: Always fetch Cc header when building reply-all. The gmail_to
pat-2026-04-21-015.pattern: Always fetch Cc header when building reply-all. The gmail_tool.py draft command now does this correctly.
pat-2026-04-21-015.problem: cmd_draft_reply only fetched From/To/Subject/Message-ID headers — never Cc. Alex Melikian was silently dropped from every reply on the Shalom BBVA thread.

pat-2026-04-21-016.name: ALWAYS fetch current SQL from Metabase GET api/card/{id} bef
pat-2026-04-21-016.pattern: ALWAYS fetch current SQL from Metabase GET api/card/{id} before writing any update. Never reconstruct from memory or prior context. The live question is source of truth. Also: the ad-hoc question# URL encodes SQL in base64 in the URL hash - to update it, decode existing URL, apply changes, re-encode, send new URL. Saved question and ad-hoc link are separate artifacts.
pat-2026-04-21-016.problem: Updating Metabase question 38354 - needed MXN conversion and legal_name swap. Overwrote query without reading current live state first, risking loss of to_char formatting Brian had manually added.

pat-2026-04-23-017.name: Visa is NOT a Jeeves investor. The confirmed Jeeves investor
pat-2026-04-23-017.pattern: Visa is NOT a Jeeves investor. The confirmed Jeeves investor list is: CRV, Tencent, Andreessen Horowitz, GIC, Stanford StartX. Do not add Visa or any other investor not explicitly confirmed by Brian or a primary source document.
pat-2026-04-23-017.problem: While building the NB Diligence deck, I incorrectly listed Visa as a Jeeves investor. Brian caught it and it had to be removed.

pat-2026-04-23-018.name: data_tape_sofom.sql must include the collateral_totals CTE (
pat-2026-04-23-018.pattern: data_tape_sofom.sql must include the collateral_totals CTE (sum of collateralamountusd from dms_mysql_jeeves_raw.collateral_records grouped by companyid) and a left join on cr.companyid = lt.company_id, selecting cr.total_collateral_amount_usd. This was added on 2026-04-23 to match the elig_ee logic in eligibility.py which requires total_collateral_amount_usd.
pat-2026-04-23-018.problem: MX SOFOM BB failed with KeyError: total_collateral_amount_usd because data_tape_sofom.sql was missing the collateral_totals CTE and join that was added to data_tape.sql

pat-2026-04-24-019.name: Workaround: write CTE queries to a .sql file and execute dir
pat-2026-04-24-019.pattern: Workaround: write CTE queries to a .sql file and execute directly via psycopg2 with sslmode=require and sslrootcert=disable (same params as sql_runner pool). The validator regex needs greedy match up to last ) before SELECT keyword.
pat-2026-04-24-019.problem: sql_runner validate_sql uses non-greedy regex to strip WITH CTEs. For multi-CTE queries it stops at first ) inside the CTE body, leaving AS as first keyword and rejecting valid queries with: Only SELECT queries are allowed. Got: AS

pat-2026-04-24-020.name: ALWAYS show the full SQL executed AND include the Drive link
pat-2026-04-24-020.pattern: ALWAYS show the full SQL executed AND include the Drive link in every query response. No exceptions. Never deliver results without both.
pat-2026-04-24-020.problem: Brian asked for SQL and Drive link after every query result

pat-2026-04-27-021.name: 1) Always read user instructions first - funding_source=loc
pat-2026-04-27-021.pattern: 1) Always read user instructions first - funding_source=loc and cash source were stated upfront. 2) Fetch the reference chart image BEFORE building anything. 3) Quarterly ratio = average of 3 monthly ratios (monthly GTV / avg monthly cash), NOT summed GTV. 4) Always put raw data in the sheet alongside the chart. 5) Google Sheets API chart source ranges require explicit sheetId on EVERY source - missing sheetId=0 causes axis labels to silently not render. 6) Google Sheets API cannot set axis tick label text color via the format field - it is silently dropped. Chart backgroundColor also does not persist via the API. These must be set manually in the UI. 7) Never claim something is impossible in the API without testing it first.
pat-2026-04-27-021.problem: Building Cash vs GTV capital efficiency chart in Google Sheets

pat-2026-04-27-022.name: Always rename the merged output file to match the data date
pat-2026-04-27-022.pattern: Always rename the merged output file to match the data date before uploading. Never ask the user about it — just do it. The correct name is always Jeeves SOFOM Borrowing Base - Master - {data_date}.xlsx where data_date is the --end-date passed to build_mx.py.
pat-2026-04-27-022.problem: MX SOFOM BB merge_template.py outputs a file named after the template date (e.g. 20260423), not the data date (e.g. 20260425). I flagged this as a note instead of just fixing it automatically.

pat-2026-04-28-023.name: 1) DocuSign email links expire — trigger Send New Link on th
pat-2026-04-28-023.pattern: 1) DocuSign email links expire — trigger Send New Link on the expired page, then grab fresh link from Gmail. 2) The Completed email (subject: Completed: Here is your signed document) contains a fresher link than the original signed notification. 3) DocuSign download is two-step: click Download button to open dropdown, then click Combined PDF — expect_download() must wrap the SECOND click (Combined PDF), not the first. 4) Draft IDs with r- prefix (e.g. r-378...) may be stale/invalid; real Gmail draft IDs use r303... format without the dash.
pat-2026-04-28-023.problem: Needed to download signed PDF from DocuSign via Playwright automation
